economic_notes: Roseau River Anishinabe Limited Partnership operates Video Lotto terminal sites. 1903 Trust has RBC loan of $12,800,000 outstanding as at March 31, 2020. COVID-19 noted as significant event. Community Development Account receives annual allotment from Roseau River 1903 Trust.
notes: Audit is qualified on three grounds: (1) Ginew Housing Authority Inc. financial records not available; (2) supporting documentation for band-owned housing unit expenditures not available; (3) tangible capital asset historical cost and disposal documentation not available. Prior period adjustments of $1,213,746 made to opening accumulated surplus and net financial assets. The First Nation is in net debt position of $1,845,987. Roseau River 1903 Trust has a $12,800,000 RBC loan guaranteed by the First Nation. Five entities accounted for by modified equity method (GBEs). Nine entities fully consolidated. No schedule of remuneration provided in extracted text. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC $7,636,460 + ISC recoveries -$24,667 + FNIHB $4,809,783 + CMHC $226,661 + Province of Manitoba $1,752,116 + FPDI $487,790 + FPDI recoveries -$20,918 = $14,867,225; however FPDI may not be government — adjusted to ISC+FNIHB+CMHC+Province = $14,399,957 treating FPDI as non-government.
